title: carlina brachylepis
intro: Carlina brachylepis, also known as the small carline thistle, is a plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its leaves and roots. The medicinal properties of Carlina brachylepis have been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. The plant is typically prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
